courseName: Core Concepts in Agricultural and Applied Economics

courseInfo.description: A rigorous introduction to the economic concepts and quantitative methods used in agricultural and resource economics. Topics include supply and demand analysis, optimization, consumer and producer theory, market equilibrium, welfare economics, trade policy, and decision under uncertainty. R is used throughout for analysis and visualization.

outlineContent:
COVER:
ECON4002: Core Concepts in Agricultural and Applied Economics
University of Western Australia
Semester 1, 2026
Instructor: Atakelty Hailu

DESCRIPTION:
A rigorous introduction to the economic concepts and quantitative methods used in agricultural and resource economics. Topics include supply and demand analysis, optimization, consumer and producer theory, market equilibrium, welfare economics, trade policy, and decision under uncertainty. R is used throughout for analysis and visualization.

CONTACTS:
Instructor: Atakelty Hailu
Email: atakelty.hailu@uwa.edu.au
Office: Social Sciences Building 380
Office Hours: Wednesdays 2–4pm or by appointment

TOPICS:
Week 1: Foundations — Economics & R Setup
Week 2: Mathematical Methods I — Linear Algebra
Week 3: Mathematical Methods II — Calculus & Functions
Week 4: Optimization — Unconstrained and Constrained
Week 5: Consumer Theory I — Utility & Marshallian Demand
Week 6: Consumer Theory II — Duality & Welfare
Week 7: Producer Theory I — Technology & Costs
Week 8: Producer Theory II — Profit Maximization & Industry
Week 9: Market Equilibrium Analysis
Week 10: International Trade and Policy
Week 11: Welfare Economics — Measuring Economic Well-Being
Week 12: Decision Under Uncertainty

ASSESSMENTS:
Homework 1: 2.5% — due Mon 16 March (GitHub: hw01-{username})
Assignment 1: 15% — due Wed 25 March (GitHub: assign01-{username})
Homework 2: 2.5% — due Mon 13 April (GitHub: hw02-{username})
Homework 3: 2.5% — due Mon 27 April (GitHub: hw03-{username})
Assignment 2: 20% — due Wed 6 May (GitHub: assign02-{username})
Homework 4: 2.5% — due Wed 13 May (GitHub: hw04-{username})
Assignment 3: 20% — due Wed 20 May (GitHub: assign03-{username})
Class Participation: 10% — continuous (attendance + homework)
Final Test Practice: ungraded — due Wed 27 May (GitHub: final-practice-{username})
Final Test: 35% — exam period 30 May – 13 June (exact date TBA)

All assignments are submitted individually via GitHub. Open the Worksheets tab in Nomad or visit github.com/ECON4002-2026-S1 to find your personal repository for each assessment. Late submissions incur a 5% per day penalty for the first 7 days, after which the assignment is not accepted.

lectureNotes.title: Week 1: Foundations — Economics & R Setup
lectureNotes.description: Introduction to formal economic analysis, R setup, basic commands, and supply-demand models

lectureNotes.title: Getting Started with R and RStudio
lectureNotes.description: Step-by-step guide to installing and configuring R, RStudio, and key packages

notebooks.title: Week 1 R Lab: Foundations — Economics & R Setup
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 1

exercises.title: Week 1 Practice Questions
exercises.description: Foundational economics concepts and R basics practice problems

additionalReadings.title: R Code Reference: Foundations
additionalReadings.description: Essential R code snippets for Week 1 — arithmetic, variables, functions, and basic economic models

lectureNotes.title: Week 2: Mathematical Methods I — Linear Algebra
lectureNotes.description: Vectors, matrices, systems of equations, and linear economic models

notebooks.title: Week 2 R Lab: Mathematical Methods I — Linear Algebra
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 2

exercises.title: Week 2 Practice Questions
exercises.description: Linear algebra and matrix operations practice

additionalReadings.title: R Code Reference: Linear Algebra
additionalReadings.description: R code for matrix operations, solving linear systems, and visualization

lectureNotes.title: Week 3: Mathematical Methods II — Calculus & Functions
lectureNotes.description: Derivatives, optimization conditions, comparative statics, and economic applications

notebooks.title: Week 3 R Lab: Mathematical Methods II — Calculus & Functions
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 3

additionalReadings.title: R Code Reference: Calculus
additionalReadings.description: R code for numerical differentiation, function plots, and optimization

lectureNotes.title: Week 4: Optimization — Unconstrained and Constrained
lectureNotes.description: First- and second-order conditions, Lagrangian method, Kuhn-Tucker conditions

notebooks.title: Week 4 R Lab: Optimization — Unconstrained and Constrained
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 4

additionalReadings.title: R Code Reference: Optimization
additionalReadings.description: R code for numerical optimization, constraint handling, and economic examples

5.title: Consumer Theory I — Utility & Marshallian Demand

lectureNotes.title: Week 5: Consumer Theory I — Utility & Marshallian Demand
lectureNotes.description: Preferences, utility functions, budget constraint, and utility maximization

notebooks.title: Week 5 R Lab: Consumer Theory I — Utility & Marshallian Demand
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 5

exercises.title: Week 5 Practice Questions
exercises.description: Consumer utility maximization and demand derivation

additionalReadings.title: R Code Reference: Consumer Theory I
additionalReadings.description: R code for utility functions, indifference curves, and demand analysis

lectureNotes.title: Week 6: Consumer Theory II — Duality & Welfare
lectureNotes.description: Expenditure minimization, Hicksian demand, duality, and welfare measures

notebooks.title: Week 6 R Lab: Consumer Theory II — Duality & Welfare
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 6

exercises.title: Week 6 Practice Questions
exercises.description: Duality theory and welfare measurement exercises

additionalReadings.title: R Code Reference: Consumer Theory II
additionalReadings.description: R code for expenditure functions, compensated demand, and welfare calculations

lectureNotes.title: Week 7: Producer Theory I — Technology & Costs
lectureNotes.description: Production functions, cost minimization, and cost curves

notebooks.title: Week 7 R Lab: Producer Theory I — Technology & Costs
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 7

exercises.title: Week 7 Practice Questions

8.title: Producer Theory II — Profit Maximization & Industry

lectureNotes.title: Week 8: Producer Theory II — Profit Maximization & Industry
lectureNotes.description: Profit maximization, supply curves, and industry equilibrium

notebooks.title: Week 8 R Lab: Producer Theory II — Profit Maximization & Industry
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 8

lectureNotes.title: Week 9: Market Equilibrium Analysis
lectureNotes.description: Competitive equilibrium, comparative statics, and market interventions

notebooks.title: Week 9 R Lab: Market Equilibrium Analysis
notebooks.description: Interactive R notebook with code snippets, exercises, and pre-baked outputs for Week 9
